summary: Russian forces are deploying the Vector X-120 kamikaze drone for targeting personnel, while Ukrainian operators are intercepting Russian drones using systems like Elka and conducting FPV anti-drone operations. Ukrainian FPV pilots are adapting tactics, including using log-periodic antennas on Molniyas to counter jamming and improve range, and employing fiber-optic-linked drones in attacks, such as on the US Victory Base in Iraq. Russian forces report high FPV kill counts, including multiple Vector and Domaha drones, and are experimenting with humanoid systems like the Phantom MK-1. Ukrainian forces are also using advanced FPV tactics, such as striking dismounted troops and countering Russian infantry with coordinated drone strikes.
